perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, as part of the consideration of the full and timely payment of the Sponsor Fees, Club hereby grants to Sponsor, and Sponsor hereby accepts, solely in the Territory, and during the Term: (i) the right to use the Benefits set forth on Exhibit A and the license and right to use the Team Marks solely in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Sponsor's Dick's Wings and Grill branded restaurants (the "Sponsor Business") in accordance with this Agreement; and (ii) the right to use the designation "Official Wings of the Jacksonville Jaguars" and such other designations as Club and Sponsor may agree to in a writing from time to time (collectively, the "Official Designations"), solely in connection with the Sponsor Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, as part of the consideration of the full and timely payment of the Sponsorship Payments, Club hereby grants to Sponsor, and Sponsor hereby accepts, solely in the Territory, and during the Term: (i) the right to use the Benefits set forth on Exhibit A and the license and right to use the Team Marks solely in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Sponsor's Dick's Wings and Grill branded restaurants (the "Sponsor Business") in accordance with this Agreement; and (ii) the right to use the designation "Official Wings of the Jacksonville Jaguars" and such other designations as Club and Sponsor may agree to in a writing from time to time (collectively, the "Official Designations"), solely in connection with the Sponsor Business.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Sponsor Fees' is changed to 'Sponsorship Payments'. The contract uses the term 'Sponsor Fees' later on in Section 3(a), so by using a new term here, we create confusion about whether the 'Sponsorship Payments' is different from the 'Annual Fee' and 'Playoff Payments' mentioned in Section 3, specifically the contradicted location, since the fees are to be reffered as 'Annual Fee'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) In consideration for the Benefits, during each Contract Year of the Term, Sponsor shall pay Club, in accordance with this Section 3(a) and Section 2(d) of the Terms and Conditions, the amount set forth next to the applicable Contract Year below (the "Annual Fee"). First Contract Year (2018/19): $ 200,000 Second Contract Year (2019/20): $ 204,000 Third Contract Year (2020/21): $ 208,080 Fourth Contract Year (2021/22): $ 212,240 Fifth Contract Year (2022/23): $ 216,490 Sponsor shall pay Club the Annual Fee for each Contract Year of this Agreement in six (6) equal installments, each due on or prior to the 1st of each month between June and November of the applicable Contract Year.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Sponsor Fees' to 'Sponsorship Payments' introduces a misaligned terminology, creating a contradiction. Section 3(a) specifically refers to the 'Annual Fee'. By changing the term in the first excerpt, it becomes unclear whether 'Sponsorship Payments' is equivalent to the 'Annual Fee,' or is something separate, leading to confusion about the amounts Sponsor is obligated to pay and contradicting the terms laid out in Section 3(a) regarding payment of the 'Annual Fee'.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall pay Club the Annual Fee for each Contract Year of this Agreement in six (6) equal installments, each due on or prior to the 1st of each month between June and November of the applicable Contract Year.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall pay Club the Annual Remuneration for each Contract Year of this Agreement in six (6) equal installments, each due on or prior to the 1st of each month between June and November of the applicable Contract Year.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Annual Fee' to 'Annual Remuneration' creates confusion. The contract refers to 'Annual Fee' consistently. If the 'Annual Remuneration' is something else, then it creates confusion, in terms of its payment method, since section 3(b) depends on this one, where is talking about 'Annual Trade Value', if this remuneration affects the trade value.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) In addition to the Annual Fees identified in Section 3(a) above, Sponsor shall provide Club with food, beverage and serving products from Sponsor's Dicks' Wings restaurant with values equal to the following (each, an "Annual Trade Value"): First Contract Year (2018/19): $ 35,000 Second Contract Year (2019/20): $ 35,700 Third Contract Year (2020/21): $ 36,410 Fourth Contract Year (2021/22): $ 37,140 Fifth Contract Year (2022/23): $ 37,890 As part of the Annual Trade Value, Sponsor shall provide Club with a designated liaison who will coordinate the menu and quantities to be provided by Sponsor. Sponsor shall deliver the food (the cost of which is included in the Annual Trade Value) to the Stadium at the time and location specified by Club. If any portion of the Annual Trade Value is not used in any given Contract Year, such unused amount shall carry forward to the subsequent Contract Year. If any portion of the Annual Trade Value is not used at the end of the Term, Club shall be permitted to use such unused amount within twelve (12) months following expiration of this Agreement. The parties acknowledge that the Annual Trade Value is inclusive of any taxes, surcharges or related fees applicable to the orders placed by Club during the Term.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Annual Fee' to 'Annual Remuneration' creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text specifically refers to 'Annual Fees' in Section 3(a) and builds upon this definition to establish the 'Annual Trade Value'. If 'Annual Remuneration' is now being used instead of 'Annual Fee', it creates uncertainty about which payment is being referenced and how the 'Annual Trade Value' is calculated, causing confusion and potential disputes regarding the contract's financial obligations. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice or other communication under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be considered given when delivered personally or by electronic mail (confirmed by one of the other permissible methods of giving notice hereunder), one business day after being sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier, or three business days after being mailed by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid and return receipt requested, to the parties at the following addresses (or at such other address as a party may specify by notice to the other):
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice or other communication under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be considered given when delivered personally or by email (confirmed by one of the other permissible methods of giving notice hereunder), one business day after being sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier, or three business days after being mailed by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid and return receipt requested, to the parties at the following addresses (or at such other address as a party may specify by notice to the other):
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'electronic mail' to 'email' introduces ambiguity. While commonly understood, 'email' is less formal and doesn't explicitly guarantee the same legal standing as 'electronic mail,' particularly if the receiving party uses this subtle distinction to claim non-receipt or challenge enforceability. The term should be exactly the same in the section 6, when providing the adresses, in the email section. It introduces uncertainty about which type of address is correct.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To Sponsor: The ARC Group, Inc. To Club: Jacksonville Jaguars, LLC 6327-4 Argyle Forest Blvd. 1 EverBank Field Drive Jacksonville, Florida 32244 Jacksonville, Florida 32202 Attn: Rick Akam Attn: Scott Massey Title: CEO Senior Vice President, Corporate Partnerships Email: rick@dickswings.com masseys@nfl.jaguars.com With a copy to: _________________________ With a copy to: Jacksonville Jaguars, LLC _________________________ 1 EverBank Field Drive _________________________ Jacksonville, Florida 32202 Attn: ____________________ Attn: Megha Parekh Title: ____________________ Senior Vice President, Chief Legal Officer Email: ____________________ parekhm@nfl.jaguars.com Notwithstanding the foregoing, delivery of an invoice via solely electronic mail shall constitute sufficient delivery under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'electronic mail' to 'email' introduces a potential ambiguity. The contradicted text uses 'electronic mail' and then 'email' interchangeably, specifically stating 'delivery of an invoice via solely electronic mail shall constitute sufficient delivery'. The modified text's use of the less formal 'email' introduces uncertainty about whether 'email' as used in the address section is equivalent to 'electronic mail' for the purposes of invoice delivery, creating a contradiction in how notices are to be regarded in the agreement.
